messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Statistics in R with Tidyverse - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Statistics in R with Tidyverse - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is offering a 4-day workshop on Statistics in R with Tidyverse <https://instats.org/seminar/statistics-using-the-tidyverse-in-r2?utm_source=r-sig-ecology> with professor Chester Ismay, running October 15, 17, 22, and 24. This comprehensive seminar introduces participants to key concepts and practices in statistical inference and data science using R, covering topics such as data wrangling, exploratory data analysis, regression analysis, resampling, empirical confidence intervals, hypothesis testing, and more. Attendees will gain hands-on experience, enhancing their ability to manage and analyze complex datasets and effectively communicate their research findings. Starting with an introduction to R and the tidyverse, the workshop will incrementally introduce statistical concepts and practical hands-on techniques for efficiently using the tidyverse to manipulate and analyze data, as well as present results with data visualizations. The workshop will also cover more advanced topics such as resampling methods for hypothesis tests with regression analysis, providing robust tools for data analysis and interpretation. Moreover, learning to communicate statistical findings effectively will enable researchers to present their work compellingly for academic journals, conferences, and policy papers.

For anyone wanting a deep dive into Quarto to automatically create reproducible research using R, we also have a 1-day workshop Quarto in RStudio: Writing Reproducible & Dynamic Research Papers <https://instats.org/seminar/quarto-in-r-studio-writing-reproducible2?utm_source=r-sig-ecology>, running Oct 7, by professor Seraphine Maerz.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/statistics-using-the-tidyverse-in-r2?utm_source=r-sig-ecology> for to secure your spot in these unique workshops and please feel free to tell your friends and colleagues!


Best wishes

?Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
instats.org <http://instats.org?utm_source=r-sig-ecology>
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Network Analysis in R - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Network Analysis in R - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is offering a 2-day seminar From Basic to Advanced Network Analysis in R <https://instats.org/seminar/from-basic-to-advanced-network-analysis-6>, running Oct 17 - 18 by professor Antonio Zinilli. This seminar offers a comprehensive understanding of network analysis and its application in R. Tailored for PhD students, academics, and professional researchers, the workshop provides a strong theoretical foundation and hands-on experience in analyzing and?visualizing complex networks in socio-economic and health contexts, equipping participants with practical skills needed to apply network analysis in their own research. Professor Zinilli has extensive experience and expertise in network analysis, and will lead participants through the underlying theory of networks coupled with a large variety of hands-on applications, demonstrating the value of network analysis for academic research across multiple fields. The workshop comes with 30 days of access to the recordings and Q&A forum access with professor Zinilli.

We also have a new 5-day Computational Network Science Using Python <https://instats.org/seminar/computational-network-science-using-pyth2>, running Nov 18 - 22 by professor Moses Boudourides, for those who prefer a computational approach using Python.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/from-basic-to-advanced-network-analysis-6> to secure your spot, and please feel free to tell your friends and colleagues about this unique opportunity.


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
instats.org <http://instats.org>

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Last chance: Using ChatGPT for Advanced Data Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Using ChatGPT for Advanced Data Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance to register for our 2-day workshops on Using ChatGPT for Advanced Data Analysis 3.0 <https://instats.org/seminar/using-chatgpt-for-advanced-data-analysis10-2> on October 19 and 22. Imagine performing an entire statistical analysis without writing a single line of code or even knowing how to use a specific statistics program. In today's world of AI based language models such as ChatGPT, plain English is sufficient. In this workshop, professor Peter Gruber will show how to use ChatGPT and its Advanced Data Analysis tool for data wrangling and statistical analysis, without needing to use any statistics programs or programming languages - and with the newest version of ChatGPT, it's even faster and more intuitive. The seminar covers a range of topics from data preparation and descriptive statistics to regression analysis, advanced statistical tests, and data visualisations. Emphasis is placed on understanding the workings and limits of this and similar AI models, while using them as extremely capable research assistants to drastically speed up and improve your research workflows.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/using-chatgpt-for-advanced-data-analysis10-2> for this unique workshop, and please feel?free to tell your friends and colleagues!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
instats.org <http://instats.org?utm_source=GEO-TECTONICS>
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Last chance: Using ChatGPT for Advanced Data Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Using ChatGPT for Advanced Data Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi Jeff

Thanks for getting in touch. As academics ourselves, we're hosting this
seminar as part of our efforts to reduce barriers to stats education. By
allowing participants to focus on statistical principles rather than coding
formalities, we've found ChatGPT (and Copilot) to be incredible tools for
decreasing the workloads of folks who are new to stats in particular - but
really anyone wanting to increase their efficiency can benefit.

Typically when teaching we usually have to teach statistics +
coding/software. By using ChatGPT the purpose of the seminar is to show how
this latter component can be drastically streamlined. This leads to people
being more interested in learning statistics rather than overwhelmed.

I hope this puts it in some context and thanks again.


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
*instats.org* <http://instats.org>

Indeed.
The posting by Michael Zyphur advertizing their courses to
*several* R-SIG-* mailing lists *has* been inappropriate
and he has been told so several times in the past.

I have now started to disallow / ban such posts.

One _single_ posting to the general  R list, R-help may be
appropriate if it happens only every few months.

Everything else has been inappropriate.

Martin Maechler
- R Core team
- admin of current R-*@r-project.org mailing lists.
